item.title: Energy Consumption of Neighboring Student Houses
item.description:
This app allows you to explore the energy consumption of two neighboring student houses (i.e., The Greenhouse and The Phi Society of 1883) at the University of the South in relation to regional consumption averages according to the U.S. Energy Information Administration. Learn what student practices potentially contribute to consumption and conservation.

Visit the github repo

HERE

item.title: The Argument for Cross-Sectoral Decoupling
item.description:
This book explores whether economic growth can continue without driving carbon emissions. It introduces the concept of ‘decoupling’ and argues how tailored innovation and infrastructural change across sectors can lead toward a greener economy.

To read this book, visit the github repo:

HERE

item.title: Does Expenditure Equal Protection?
item.description:
This data story examines the relationship between endemic, endangered species (i.e., mammals and fish) and environmental protection expenditures across countries through the lens of the 14th and 15th Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), Life Below Water and Life on Land. Some of the questions addressed in this data story include: Do governments adjust their expenditures based on biodiversity loss? How does government spending align with commitments to Life Below Water and Life on Land? What can you do to promote biodiversity conservation?

View data story:

HERE

or visit the github repo:

HERE

item.title: Rush Hour Traffic on University
item.description:
The following data story explores the data found using a data entry app of my creation. The data entry app allows the user to log traffic data on University Avenue in Sewanee, Tennessee. Using the interactive graph, explore how certain cars drive during Sewanee rush hour.

View data story:

HERE

or visit github repo:

HERE

item.title: Renewable Energy Insights
item.description:
This data story explores data from the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), revealing various insights through interactive plots. If you’re curious about who’s driving solar affordability, how much solar is producing for Japan’s economy, or how Ireland has experienced a declining global warming rate, read the follow data story:

HERE

or visit the github repo:

HERE
